_meta.method: Iterated BadBunnyvocabulary_wikt.index.json in index order (deck-rank order), keeping entries where bench_deck_quality.visible(wikt_master, idx) is True, until 500 visible wikt cards were collected (rank = position in that visible-only sequence). For each, matched the live deck's visible set on (word.lower(), lemma.lower()) and compared top gloss (real_senses(master)[0]['translation']), case-insensitive/stripped. Rows emitted only where a live match exists AND the top gloss differs. `suggestion` is a reviewer hint, not a verdict: a first mechanical pass flagged near-synonym/formatting-only diffs as 'cosmetic' via string/token similarity (SequenceMatcher ratio >= 0.6, substring, or Jaccard token overlap >= 0.34); the remaining ~51 rows (plus one mechanical false positive, 'carro') were hand-reviewed using Spanish/English lexical judgment and the wikt/live all-senses lists as corroborating evidence (see suggestion_note per row).

source_files.wikt_master: Artists/spanish/vocabulary_master_wikt.json (read from scratchpad copy per concurrent-writer isolation)

entries.suggestion_note: manual review: 'que' cannot mean bare 'to' on its own; wikt's 'that' is the standard gloss and also appears (non-top) in live's own menu.
manual review: 'la' as DET 'the' is the primary/default sense of the article; live's top pick 'it' (object pronoun use) is valid but secondary.

manual review: wikt's only sense is 'bishop' (NOUN, board-games context) for the preposition 'a' -- POS mismatch (live is ADP) and a chess-notation homograph, not the preposition. No alt sense in wikt menu to promote.
manual review: 'de' most commonly renders as 'of' (genitive); live's top 'from' is valid but less central.

manual review: 'una' as DET: live picked 'a' (indefinite article), wikt picked 'one' (numeral) with 'an; a' present as a non-top alt in wikt's OWN menu. Both are extremely common senses of una; genuinely context-dependent.

entries.suggestion_note: manual review: 'hay' (impersonal haber) really means 'there is/are'; neither gloss captures that, but wikt's 'have; forms the perfect aspect' is a bare grammar-book description of haber-as-auxiliary, less useful than live's 'to have to' (tied to the real hay-que idiom).
manual review: 'nadie' is literally 'no one/nobody'; live's 'anybody' is a double-negative-avoidance translation artifact, not the base gloss.

entries.suggestion_note: manual review: 'gustar' commonly = 'to like'; wikt's 'to experiment, to try, to taste' is a real but archaic/rare sense. Gemini (flash-lite-wiktionary) actually classified this one and still picked wrong -- likely because 'to like' isn't offered as a distinct candidate in the wikt sense menu for this lemma (see Task 2 method-priority note).

manual review: 'carajo' is overwhelmingly used as an interjection ('fuck/damn/hell') in lyrics; wikt picked the literal anatomical NOUN sense ('cock'). live's own menu has NOUN 'dick' at a non-top rank -- wikt's pick corresponds to that minor sense, not the dominant interjection use.
